commit.message:
Implementer retry-then-fail for ferdigstill og feilregistrer (#12)

* Implement retry-then-fail for ferdigstill og feilregistrer (#11)

- Add FEILET_FERDIGSTILL and FEILET_FEILREGISTRER to Status enum
- Add optional feilmelding field to EuxSedJournalstatusPut
- FerdigstillJournalposterService: on first failure set FEILET_FERDIGSTILL
  with feilmelding, on second failure set KORRUPT. Process retry batch
  first to prevent same-run double processing.
- FeilregistrerJournalposterService: restructure from mapNotNull chain to
  per-item try/catch so failures trigger status updates. Same retry-then-
  fail pattern with FEILET_FEILREGISTRER.
- SakUtenFerdigstilteJournalposterException remains exempt (business
  precondition, not permanent failure)
- On success, feilmelding=null clears any previous error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- FerdigstillRetryApiImplTest: verify second failure → KORRUPT with
  feilmelding, and happy-path items still get JOURNALFOERT
- FeilregistrerRetryApiImplTest: verify second failure → KORRUPT with

- Log all incoming headers for debugging

commit.message:
feat(copyTeamPermissions): remove allAssets DEV-1333 DEV-1334 (#6893)

### 📣 Summary

Make Copy Team Permissions feature properly load all available projects
and allow for searching particular one within the project selection
dropdown. Also stop using deprecated `allAssets` store.

### 💭 Notes

Changes here:
- Changed `CopyTeamPermissions` to functional component
- Use infinite scroll in project selection dropdown to load as many
projects as user needs (through `useInfiniteScroll` and
`InfiniteScrollTrigger`)
- `SharingForm` passes on whole locally available asset object down to
`CopyTeamPermissions` and doesn't rely on `allAssets` anymore

### 👀 Preview steps

1. ℹ️ Have an account, multiple different users, and a three projects A,
B, and C
2. Never open project C
3. Open Project A → Settings → Sharing
4. Share project with some users
5. Open Project B → Settings → Sharing
6. Notice the "Who has access" section displays single user (project
owner)
7. Toggle open "Copy team from another project"
8. 🔴 [on main] Notice that "Select source project…" dropdown only lists
project A
9. 🟢 [on PR] Notice that "Select source project…" dropdown lists all
your projects
10. Select project A
11. Click "copy" and then confirm with "PROCEED"
12. 🟢 Notice a success notification "permissions were copied
successfully"
13. 🟢 Notice the "Who has access" section now displays all the users
from point (4)

---------

Co-authored-by: romanett <7413710+romanett@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix parallel HTTPS test isolation: replace static Startup.Listener singleton with per-port ConcurrentDictionary

Root cause: HttpsTransportListener.Startup.Listener was a static singleton that was overwritten
each time a new HTTPS server started. With parallel fixture execution (ClientTest, ClientBatchTest,
NodeCacheAsyncTest etc. all running their https/opc.https instances simultaneously), the last
server to start captured the global Listener. Requests arriving at any earlier server's Kestrel
instance were then dispatched to the wrong listener, causing BadServerHalted / NullReferenceException
cascade failures.

Fix: Replace Startup.Listener (static singleton) with Startup.Listeners (ConcurrentDictionary<int,
HttpsTransportListener> keyed by port). Start() registers the listener by port; Dispose() removes it.
The Kestrel middleware now routes each request to the correct listener using context.Connection.LocalPort.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/OPCFoundation/UA-.NETStandard/sessions/a2a66689-1c27-4834-ab0b-32a2a0974fd6

Co-authored-by: romanett <7413710+romanett@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add defensive try-catch in Dispose for Listeners.TryRemove per code review

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/OPCFoundation/UA-.NETStandard/sessions/a2a66689-1c27-4834-ab0b-32a2a0974fd6

Co-authored-by: romanett <7413710+romanett@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove static Startup.Listeners; inject HttpsTransportListener via IWebHostBuilder DI

Register the listener instance in the web host DI container via
IWebHostBuilder.ConfigureServices(services => services.AddSingleton(this)).
Startup.Configure receives it as a method-injected parameter, eliminating all
static state from the Startup class.

Constructor injection was considered but doesn't work on .NET 8+ where HostBuilder
activates Startup from the host-level service provider, which does not include
services registered via IWebHostBuilder.ConfigureServices. Method injection in
Configure() always resolves from the web-host app container, working correctly on
all target frameworks.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/OPCFoundation/UA-.NETStandard/sessions/108c8840-5555-4c12-83fd-91ed0990d531

commit.message:
feat(maglev): true-inline array load via monomorphic stack IC

Rewrite emit_inline_load_keyed_smi to emit ~15 x86 instructions on the
hot path with ZERO function calls for Smi/Boolean/undefined/null element
loads.  A 3-slot inline cache (handle, data_ptr, len) on the JIT stack
caches the array's Vec buffer pointer and length.

Fast path (IC hit):
  - Compare heap handle → IC handle (1 cmp+jne)
  - Unsigned bounds check: key < len (1 cmp+jae, catches negatives)
  - Compute element address: data_ptr + key * 24
  - Load discriminant byte, dispatch to Smi/Bool/Undef/Null
  - Smi: movsxd sign-extend i32 payload
  - Bool: movzx + and 1 + add JIT_FALSE

Slow path (IC miss):
  - Calls new jit_runtime_inline_load_keyed_smi_ic_r15 which fills the
    IC AND performs the load in one call, then falls through to the
    generic stub if needed.

Supporting changes:

MacroAssembler (masm_x64.rs):
  - Add lea_base_disp32, movzx_byte_base_disp32, movsxd_base_disp32
  - Add CondCode::AboveEq and CondCode::Below for unsigned comparisons

Runtime (baseline/compiler.rs):
  - Add probe_jsvalue_layout(): discovers JsValue byte layout at runtime
    (discriminant offset, Smi/Bool payload offsets) via byte scanning
  - Add JsValueLayout struct for the probed layout
  - Add jit_runtime_fill_array_ic_r15: resolves heap handle to Vec buffer
  - Add jit_runtime_inline_load_keyed_smi_ic_r15: combined IC fill + load

Codegen (maglev/codegen.rs):
  - Add array_ic_base and total_cache_bytes fields to MaglevCodegen
  - Prologue/epilogue now reserve and zero-init the IC alongside the
    existing monomorphic call cache
  - Store path invalidates IC (zeroes handle) after every keyed store
    to guard against Vec reallocation
  - Cache JsValueLayout via OnceLock for process-lifetime reuse

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix get_cox_pairwise_df returning NA hazard ratios when arm has >2 levels (#194) `get_cox_pairwise_df` always reads row `1` from `coxph_ans$conf.int`, but when the arm factor has >2 levels, subsetting the data doesn't drop unused factor levels — so `conf.int` still contains a row per non-reference level (with `NA` for absent ones). Only the first comparison returns valid results; all subsequent ones return `NA`. ## Fix Replace the hard-coded row index `1` with `paste0(arm, current_arm)`, matching R's factor dummy variable naming convention: ```r # Before — always reads first row regardless of which arm is current hr = sprintf("%.2f", coxph_ans$conf.int[1, 1]) # After — reads the row for the current comparison arm conf_int_row <- paste0(arm, current_arm) hr = sprintf("%.2f", coxph_ans$conf.int[conf_int_row, 1]) ``` ## Tests Added `test-get_cox_pairwise_df.R` covering: - 3-arm case returns non-`NA` HR and CI for all comparisons (the regression case) - 2-arm case continues to work - Default `ref_group` falls back to first factor level - Input validation errors
Original prompt > > ---- > > *This section details on the original issue you should resolve* > > get_cox_pairwise_df returns hazard ratio of NA if by variable has more than two levels > The issue resides in the get_cox_pairwise_df function. The idea of this function is that if your by variable has n levels, it computes n-1 cox proportional hazards model, each time comparing one level to the reference level. However, the function is only working correctly if you have two levels. If you have more than two levels, all comparisons after the first one will return NA for the hazard ratio and confidence interval. > > The reason for this is that the function initiates a for loop and in each iteration, subsets the data to just two levels (reference and one other) and then computes the cox model. However, because the by variable is a factor it still remembers that the other levels exists so the cox proportional hazard model will still include all levels in the summary with a hazard ratio of NA. This is fine as long as we are careful to return the correct row in the output but the function is always returning the first row of the summary when in fact, it should return the ith row for the ith iteration of the for loop. > > In simple terms, we need to update rows 104, 107 and 109 of the [function ](https://github.com/insightsengineering/crane/blob/main/R/get_cox_pairwise_df.R) to reference the ith iteration rather than hard-coding this to 1, highlighted below: > > > > follow instruction from @reesnj to propose the PR > > ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section) > > > >
- Fixes insightsengineering/crane#171 --- 📱 Kick off Copilot coding agent tasks wherever you are with [GitHub Mobile](https://gh.io/cca-mobile-docs), available on iOS and Android. --------- Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: shajoezhu <3692541+shajoezhu@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: Davide Garolini Co-authored-by: Jan Szczypiński Co-authored-by: Jan Szczypiński

commit.message:
Enhance role-based access control and add permission tests (#3653)

* Enhance role-based access control and add permission tests

- Improve ValidateRolePermissions logic and add ILogger support for detailed access denial logging
- Expand default roles (add AuthenticatedUser, TrustedApplication)
- Refine RoleBasedIdentity to merge roles and avoid duplicates
- Set UserIdentity default GrantedRoleIds to Anonymous
- Fix MasterNodeManager PrepareValidationCache
- Add comprehensive unit tests for role permission validation and logging

* Fix GDS self-admin role: restore NodeId.Null in GrantedRoleIds for ApplicationSelfAdmin

The RoleBasedIdentity constructor was filtering out roles with null RoleIds
(!role.RoleId.IsNull), which broke the GDS ApplicationSelfAdmin mechanism.
The GDS OnAddSelfAdminUserRolePermissions relies on GrantedRoleIds.Contains(NodeId.Null)
to detect self-admin users (since GdsRole.ApplicationSelfAdmin has RoleId = ExpandedNodeId.Null).

Also remove unused System.Data using statement.

commit.message:
feat: media perception, DOM retry/recovery, WebMCP invocation, handoff fix

**Media perception**
- Add `MediaKind`, `MediaReadyState`, `ObservedMedia` types to shared/perception.ts
- Add `media: ObservedMedia[]` to `PageObservation` and `PageGraph`
- `collectMedia()` in dom-extractor.ts captures video/audio elements with
  full playback state (src, title, paused, muted, volume, currentTime,
  duration, loop, readyState) across shadow DOM and iframes
- Pass media through normalize.ts into PageGraph
- Update all 12 golden fixture JSON files with `"media": []`

**DOM action retry/recovery**
- `withDomRetry()` in site-capability-registry.ts wraps all DOM tool and
  browser action execution with up to 3 attempts
- Exponential backoff (300ms → 600ms) between attempts
- Calls `waitForPageSettled()` before each retry to handle SPA re-renders
- Only retries on DOM resolution errors; logic errors fail immediately

**WebMCP invocation** (previously stubbed)
- `extractWebMcpTools()`: queries `navigator.webMcp`, `window.WebMCP`,
  and `<script type="application/webmcp+json">` to enumerate real tools
- `invokeWebMcpTool()`: invokes via `navigator.webMcp.invoke/call/run`,
  `window.WebMCP.invoke/call`, or HTTP POST to tool endpoint
- `buildWebMcpManifest()` now sets `availability: "ready"` and populates
  `tools[]` when tools are found; `retryable: true` on invocation failure
- Add `result?: unknown` to `BrowserCommandResult` completed variant

**Human handoff fix**
- Remove misleading throw in `dom-actuator.ts` `await_human` case;
  the registry intercepts this command via `createHandoff()` before
  the DOM actuator is ever reached

commit.message:
fix: auto-skip breakpoint at PC before resume/wait_halt/step_out

When PC is sitting on an active breakpoint, resume() would immediately
re-trigger the same breakpoint (elapsed=0.0s). This is a classic
debugger issue that affected wait_halt and step_out as well.

Fix: _skip_breakpoint_at_pc() temporarily removes BP, single-steps
one instruction, then re-arms BP before the actual resume.

Applied to: target.resume(), wait_halt(resume_first=True), step_out()
Updated SKILL.md with breakpoint skip mechanism documentation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
